title: Best deck cleaner of 2026: Which Lift Stains Without Harming Plants
excerpt: The best deck cleaner for your deck material and stain level, without the empty promises—pick a formula that works and won't kill your lawn.
author: Editorial Team
metaDescription: The best deck cleaner for your deck material and stain level, without the empty promises—pick a formula that works and won't kill your lawn.
intro:
A deck cleaner can make your weathered boards look new—or it can leave you scrubbing for hours with no progress. Many products claim to erase mold and mildew instantly, but real performance depends heavily on the deck's material, how long stains have set in, and whether you're spraying, power-washing, or using a brush.

That's why matching the cleaner to your specific situation—wood vs. composite, light dusting vs. deep black mold—is more important than the marketing on the bottle. Our picks focus on formulas that consistently deliver real results, breaking down the trade-offs you'll face, like choosing between fast-acting bleach and plant-safe peroxide.

And a quick tip: hose-end sprayers that come with concentrates are notoriously leaky. Transfer the solution to a dedicated pump sprayer to save product and avoid a mess.

products.title: Simple Green Oxy Solve Total Outdoor Cleaner Concentrate, Pressure Washer & Manual Use, EPA Safer Choice Certified, Peroxide Cleaner for Wood, Concrete, Vinyl & More
products.label: Simple Green Total Outdoor
products.oneLiner: Plant-safe peroxide concentrate cleans wood, composite, and concrete, covering 6,400 sq ft.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: For best results on tough stains, pre-soak the surface and use a stiff-bristle brush — the peroxide formula works better with a little agitation on organic buildup.
pros: Cleans a wide range of outdoor surfaces without bleach or chlorine — 43 mentions, 86% positive — versatility praised for wood, composite, vinyl
Leaves surfaces visibly cleaner and brighter after a single application — 36 mentions, 83% positive — decks and siding appear refreshed
Mixes and applies easily with a pressure washer foam cannon or spray bottle — 30 mentions, 93% positive — simple mix-and-spray process
cons: On heavily weathered wood with deep black mold, the cleaning power can fall short, leaving behind stains that require extra scrubbing or a stronger product — 414 mentions, 35% negative — inconsistent effectiveness on tough organic stains
products.verdict: For homeowners who want a single, plant-safe concentrate for routine deck and siding cleaning across mixed surfaces, this is the practical choice — just pair it with a pressure washer, and know that a heavily neglected deck may still need a more aggressive first pass.
products.review:
Safe around vegetation and free of bleach, Simple Green Total Outdoor uses a peroxide formula to clean wood, composite, vinyl, concrete, and more. It mixes easily with water for use in a pressure washer foam cannon or a manual spray bottle, and leaves a fresh scent instead of harsh chemical odors. One gallon of concentrate covers up to 6,400 square feet, making it a cost-effective option for routine deck and siding maintenance.

The product applies with minimal fuss and visibly brightens surfaces after a single wash. On heavily weathered wood with deeply embedded black mold or years-old organic stains, however, the cleaning power can be inconsistent — some areas may show only minor improvement compared to plain water, requiring a follow-up scrub or a stronger cleaner for full restoration.

This concentrate suits homeowners who want a single, eco-conscious cleaner for decks, driveways, furniture, and siding, and who are willing to accept a slight trade-off in stain removal for the sake of plant and pet safety. It’s a maintenance cleaner rather than a restorative one; if your deck hasn’t been cleaned in several seasons, a bleach-based alternative may be a better starting point.

💡 💡 Tip: For best results on tough stains, pre-soak the surface and use a stiff-bristle brush — the peroxide formula works better with a little agitation on organic buildup.

products.title: 30 SECONDS Outdoor Cleaner 2 Pack with Hose End Sprayer - Cleans Stains from Algae, Mold and Mildew on Vinyl Siding, Deck, Patio, Brick and More
products.label: 30 SECONDS Outdoor Cleaner
products.oneLiner: Bleach-based hose-end sprayer kills mold and algae on contact, no scrubbing required.
pros: Dissolves algae, mold, and mildew on contact — no scrubbing needed — 1713 mentions, 79% positive — works well and rapidly on algae and mildew
Simple garden hose connection; no pressure washer required — 384 mentions, 86% positive — no pressure washer needed, user-friendly
Leaves decks, siding, and patios looking dramatically renewed — 175 mentions, 88% positive — visual results are impressive
cons: The included sprayer can leak at the connector, wasting product and creating a mess — 210 mentions, 96% negative — widespread packaging flaw; many resort to using their own sprayer
products.verdict: A no-scrub bleach cleaner that transforms large outdoor areas in minutes — ideal for those comfortable with strong chemicals and the extra step of using their own sprayer.
products.review:
Unlike plant-safe, peroxide-based cleaners that often need extra elbow grease on stubborn mildew, this bleach formula dissolves organic stains on contact with a garden hose — delivering immediate results on robust wood, composite, or concrete decks without a pressure washer.

The tradeoff: it’s bleach, so you must protect nearby landscaping meticulously, and plan to supply your own reliable hose-end sprayer instead of the one in the box. This makes the 30 SECONDS cleaner best for those with large, durable surfaces who value fast, no-scrub cleaning and are prepared to manage the chemical and sprayer limitations.

products.title: STAR BRITE Outdoor Collection All Surface Mold Stain & Mildew Stain Remover Spray - Cleans & Removes Stains on Contact - 32 OZ Spray (054432)
products.label: Star Brite Mold & Mildew Spray
products.oneLiner: Spray-on, rinse-off mildew remover safe for Sunbrella, awnings, and outdoor upholstery.
products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: Always use in a well-ventilated outdoor area and wear a mask and gloves to keep the bleach odor manageable.
pros: Removes mold and mildew stains often in a single application, making fabrics look new. — 530 mentions, 90% positive — praised for making cushions and fabrics look brand new
No scrubbing needed — just spray, wait, and rinse with a garden hose. — 78 mentions, 86% positive — easy process only needing a garden hose
Safe for Sunbrella and outdoor acrylics, won't discolor threads or fabric. — 209 mentions, 72% positive — mildew removal without discoloration
cons: Strong bleach odor requires good ventilation and protective gear. — 64 mentions, 80% negative — pervasive chemical smell can be overwhelming in enclosed areas
products.verdict: Ideal for targeted mold and mildew removal on outdoor fabrics where scrubbing isn't an option, but plan for ventilation and protective gear to handle the bleach smell.
products.review:
Star Brite lifts deep-seated mildew stains from cushions, awnings, and Sunbrella with a simple spray-and-rinse approach, leaving threads and stitching intact. It works on contact for spot treatments, not as a whole-deck wash or for large wood surfaces.

Boat owners and anyone managing delicate outdoor textiles will appreciate the no-scrub convenience. The bleach odor is strong and may overwhelm without good airflow; wear a mask and gloves, and avoid using it in enclosed spaces.

💡 💡 Tip: Always use in a well-ventilated outdoor area and wear a mask and gloves to keep the bleach odor manageable.

products.label: Sun Joe House & Deck Cleaner

products.usageNote: 💡 Tip: For ingrained stains, pre-soak for 10–15 minutes and scrub lightly before pressure washing to boost results.
pros: Clears everyday dirt, mildew, and algae effectively from decks, siding, and driveways — 103 mentions, 77% positive
Pleasant, bleach-free scent makes pressure washing a less jarring chore — 7 mentions, 86% positive for scent
Foams well through cannons, giving visual feedback during cleaning — 6 mentions, 83% positive for foaming
products.verdict: Ideal for Sun Joe owners wanting a regular maintenance cleaner that smells good and is safe for wood and vinyl — just plan on extra elbow grease for neglected surfaces.
products.review:
Sun Joe's concentrate foams readily through foam cannons and leaves a fresh, bleach-free scent behind — a clear alternative to the sharp chemical odor of 30 Seconds bleach options. Everyday dirt, mildew, and algae lift away with minimal effort when paired with a pressure washer, making regular deck and siding maintenance quick and headache-free.

This is a maintenance cleaner, not a heavy-duty stain excavator. Homeowners doing seasonal refreshes on wood, vinyl, or composite will appreciate the gentle formula and clean smell. For set-in black stains or moss, a 10–15 minute pre-soak and some scrubbing are still needed — a fair tradeoff for avoiding bleach fumes and keeping things mild.

💡 💡 Tip: For ingrained stains, pre-soak for 10–15 minutes and scrub lightly before pressure washing to boost results.

Prepares wood well for subsequent oiling and sealing — 20 mentions, 70% positive — makes the restoration process smoother
cons: Follow-up teak oil purchase and heavier scrubbing are needed for neglected furniture — 17 mentions, 35% negative — value perception mixed, especially for small projects
products.verdict: When the project is teak, not general deck cleaning, this concentrate delivers a color revival that bleach-based washes can't—just plan to follow with oil.
products.review:
Star Brite Teak Cleaner & Brightener is a single-step solution that reverses grey, weathered teak to a natural golden color without sanding. It targets teak and fine woods specifically, unlike all-purpose cleaners that can discolor delicate grain. Teak furniture owners seeking a straightforward chemical refresh will appreciate the easy application: spread, let sit, scrub lightly, and rinse. The catch: a full restoration still requires follow-up teak oil to seal the surface, and heavily neglected pieces demand more scrubbing than the 'light scrub' description suggests.

💡 💡 Tip: Budget for a quality teak oil and apply it promptly after the cleaner has dried to lock in the restored color.

products.title: TIMBERTECH Composite DeckCleaner - 1 Gallon
products.label: TimberTech Composite Deck Cleaner
products.oneLiner: Biodegradable concentrate cleans TimberTech AZEK and PRO decking without marring or voiding warranties.
pros: Removes spots and stains from TimberTech composite with minimal scrubbing. — 51 mentions, 75% positive — visibly refreshes the deck surface
Won’t discolor or damage the capped surface when used per instructions. — 14 mentions, 71% positive — leaves a clean finish
cons: Price is substantially higher than generic composite cleaners, and for light maintenance a diluted soap mix may achieve comparable results. — 17 mentions, 71% negative — perceived as expensive for what it is
products.verdict: For TimberTech owners dealing with persistent stains or who must follow warranty guidelines, this cleaner justifies its premium; everyone else will find the top pick equally effective for routine composite care at a friendlier price.
products.review: As the only manufacturer-recommended cleaner for TimberTech’s capped composite decking, this biodegradable concentrate removes embedded spots and light organic stains with minimal scrubbing, leaving the finish unharmed. It fills a narrow but critical role: protecting the warranty on AZEK, PRO, and EDGE boards where generic peroxide or bleach cleaners might void coverage or interact unpredictably with the capstock. Unlike the top pick’s broad plant-safe approach, this formula is purpose-built for one material. The trade-off is price — at roughly twice the cost of standard composite cleaners, it’s an investment that makes sense primarily for stain-specific needs or warranty-required maintenance, not routine wash-downs.

Unlike lighter pressure washer detergents that struggle with heavy grease, this non-butyl concentrate delivers strong degreasing power at extreme dilutions. It cuts through oil and grime on concrete, machinery, and even composite decking, making it a practical choice for large-area maintenance when used in a pressure washer or scrubber.

The tradeoff is packaging: the jug can arrive with a loose lid, causing product loss. For buyers who need a budget-friendly degreaser and can handle a quick container check, the cleaning performance and low per-use cost justify the small hassle.

💡 💡 Tip: Inspect the container seal immediately upon delivery and consider transferring the soap to a sturdier jug to avoid spills during use.

Deck owners needing consistent mold removal may prefer a stronger bleach or peroxide cleaner instead.

products.verdict: This is a sensible pick for lakefront homes needing a gentle, hose-down clean for vinyl or aluminum siding — but for wood or composite deck staining, the Total Outdoor cleaner delivers more consistent results.
products.review: Simple Green Oxy Solve is a plant-safe, no-scrub siding wash that brightens vinyl and aluminum with minimal effort. The peroxide formula is safe for lakefront homes, pets, and gardens. On deck surfaces, however, it may leave stubborn mold and mildew behind, often failing to deliver the deep clean needed — a shortfall that makes the Total Outdoor cleaner a stronger deck option.

buyingGuide.heading: Cleaning Effectiveness and Stain Type
buyingGuide.body:
Bleach-based formulas (sodium hypochlorite) work rapidly on organic growth—algae, moss, mildew—by oxidizing stains on contact. That speed comes with a cost: bleach can discolor wood fibers over time and kill surrounding plants if not rinsed thoroughly.

Peroxide-based or surfactant cleaners are gentler and safer for vegetation, but they often need a dwell time of 5–10 minutes and light agitation to lift deeply embedded grime. For heavily weathered wood or black mold colonies, even the best peroxide cleaner may require a second application.

buyingGuide.heading: Surface Compatibility
buyingGuide.body: Wood decks absorb chemicals differently than composite or vinyl. Softwoods like cedar can be etched by strong acids, while composite boards usually tolerate most formulations but may discolor with prolonged bleach contact. Check the manufacturer's guidelines for your decking material—TimberTech, for example, specifies its own cleaner to protect warranties.

buyingGuide.heading: Application Method
buyingGuide.body: Pressure washer detergents use high-pressure water to boost cleaning, but the chemical must foam well and cling to vertical surfaces. Hose-end sprayers offer convenience but frequently leak at the connector; transferring the solution to a quality pump sprayer saves product and ensures even coverage. Manual brush-on methods give you the most control on stubborn spots.

buyingGuide.heading: Plant and Pet Safety
buyingGuide.body: Bleach runoff is toxic to grass, flowers, and aquatic life. When using bleach-based cleaners, pre-soak plants and rinse them immediately after application. Peroxide formulas labeled 'plant-safe' or 'EPA Safer Choice' can be applied near gardens without harm, but always avoid overspray directly onto leaves.

buyingGuide.heading: Concentration and Coverage
buyingGuide.body: Concentrates offer lower per-use cost and let you adjust dilution for light vs. heavy cleaning, but they require mixing and a separate sprayer. Ready-to-use bottles eliminate measuring but often cost more per square foot. Coverage claims like 'up to 6,400 sq ft' assume ideal conditions; rough, porous surfaces demand more product.

faq.question: Does 30 Seconds Outdoor Cleaner really work without scrubbing?
faq.answer: For light algae and mildew, yes—the bleach oxidizes organic matter on contact, and a garden hose rinse is enough. On heavily ground-in dirt or thick black mold, you may need to lightly scrub or apply a second round. It excels as a maintenance spray rather than a one-time restoration.

faq.question: How long does Wet and Forget take to clean a deck?
faq.answer: Results typically appear after several weeks, as rain and wind gradually wash away loosened debris. In dry climates or on thick stains, it can take months and multiple applications. If you need visible improvement within days, a scrubbing or pressure-washing cleaner is a better fit.

faq.question: Is Simple Green deck cleaner safe for composite decking?
faq.answer: Simple Green Oxy Solve Total Outdoor is safe for composite; its peroxide formula won't discolor or degrade capped boards. The Simple Green Deck and Fence variant is also compatible, but test a hidden spot first. Always follow your composite manufacturer's cleaning recommendations to preserve warranties.

faq.question: Can deck cleaner kill my grass if the runoff reaches the lawn?
faq.answer: Bleach-based cleaners can certainly kill grass and ornamentals if not rinsed promptly. Wet the lawn before application, then wash any overspray away within minutes. Peroxide-based, Safer Choice-certified products are much less phytotoxic, but you should still rinse overspray to be safe.
